# Application Specification 

## 1. Executive Summary

-   Describe what the application is and the problem it solves
-   Identify the primary users or consumers
-   Define the overall purpose and value of the system

----------

## 2. Architectural Context

-   Describe where the application fits within a broader ecosystem
-   Identify external systems, dependencies, and integrations
-   Define system boundaries and ownership

----------

## 3. Goals

-   Define the primary outcomes the system must achieve
-   Include measurable or testable success criteria where possible
-   Focus on business and system-level objectives

----------

## 4. Non-Goals

-   Explicitly define what the system will not do
-   Clarify boundaries to prevent scope creep
-   Exclude adjacent or commonly confused responsibilities

----------

## 5. Core Capabilities (Subsystems)

-   Identify the major functional areas of the system
-   Group related behavior into logical subsystems
-   Provide a brief description of each capability

----------

## 6. High-Level Architecture

-   Describe the overall system design (e.g., layered, event-driven, microservices)
-   Identify major components and their relationships
-   Outline data and control flow at a high level

----------

## 7. Technology Strategy

-   Define key technology choices and constraints
-   Include language, frameworks, and infrastructure approach
-   Focus on guiding principles, not implementation details

----------

## 8. Cross-Cutting Concerns

-   Define system-wide concerns such as:
    -   Security and authentication
    -   Observability (logging, tracing, metrics)
    -   Reliability and fault tolerance
-   Apply consistently across all subsystems

----------

## 9. Success Criteria

-   Define how success will be measured
-   Include functional, performance, and reliability indicators
-   Ensure criteria are observable and testable
